content.full_text:
February 26, 1981

David E. Miller  
David E. Miller, Law Corporation  
Russ Building, Suite 1823  
235 Montgomery Street  
San Francisco, CA 94104

Dear Mr. Miller:

Thank you for your letter of February 5, 1981, to the Chairman of the Fair Political Practices Commission in which you requested an opinion concerning the reporting obligation of a bona fide church or religious society under the provisions of the Political Reform Act. Because your letter raises no substantial questions involving an interpretation of the Act, no formal opinion will be issued. I hope, however, the following response will be helpful.

You asked whether a person promoting a "measure" (as defined in Government Code §82043) on behalf of a bona fide church or religious society would be exempt from the campaign disclosure provisions if the sole purpose was to protect the public right to practice the doctrines of said church (§86300(c)).

The exemption provided to a person when representing a bona fide church or religious society solely for the purpose of protecting the public right to practice the doctrine of said church under §86300(c) applies only to the lobbying disclosure provisions (Chapter 6) of the Political Reform Act. The exemption in §86300(c) does not apply to the campaign disclosure provisions (Chapter 4) of the Political Reform Act.

During a telephone conversation on February 19, 1981 with Rich Eichman of our staff, you asked if the proponent of a measure is required to disclose a campaign contribution of $100 or more received from a bona fide church or religious society. The Political Reform Act does not provide an exemption for a bona fide church or religious society with respect to campaign contributions made to candidates or measures. Furthermore, if a bona fide church or religious society were to contribute $5,000 or more in a calendar year to candidates and/or ballot measures, the church or religious society would be required to file campaign statements as a "Major Donor Committee".

David E. Miller

I hope this response has been helpful. If you need additional assistance, please do not hesitate to contact Rich Eichman at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Ted Prim
Executive Director

RE/bb
